# `legal_app/backend/team.py`

_Python module_

## Overview

Team module routes (spec §5.3): members + permissions + audit.

Phase 2.3. Three tabs in the frontend, one router here. All write operations
require the `manage` capability and the last-manage-holder safeguard kicks in
before any destructive change to roles, users, or the permission matrix.

### `list_members`

```
@router.get('/members')
```

```
list_members(user: dict = Depends(current_user), conn: sqlite3.Connection = Depends(get_db)) -> list[dict]
```

Anyone authenticated can see the roster; only `manage` can mutate it.

Phase 2.4: include `legacy_id` so the frontend MemberPicker can pass the
TEXT user id (`u1, u2, …`) to POST /api/matters/{id}/members without a
second round-trip to resolve the bridge.

_Defined at line 97._

### `invite_member`

```
@router.post('/members', status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
```

```
invite_member(body: InviteRequest, user: dict = Depends(_require_manage), conn: sqlite3.Connection = Depends(get_db)) -> dict
```

Create a user with a known initial password.

Phase 2.3 MVP keeps this simple: caller supplies the initial password (or
generates one client-side). A "magic link" invite flow can land later.

_Defined at line 194._

### `update_permission`

```
@router.patch('/permissions')
```

```
update_permission(cell: PermissionToggle, user: dict = Depends(_require_manage), conn: sqlite3.Connection = Depends(get_db)) -> list[dict]
```

Toggle one cell of the matrix. Rolls back if it would zero out manage.
